role_title: Distribution Strategy and Planning - CCG Strategy & Operations, VP - State Street Investment Management

level_signals: Proven ability to operate as a senior individual contributor or player/coach
Comp range $115K-$205K base, consistent with VP-level at a large financial institution

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of relevant experience in strategy, consulting, financial services, or asset management, with demonstrated progression in scope, influence, and impact
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative or business discipline required
Proven ability to operate as a senior individual contributor or player/coach, managing complex initiatives with significant autonomy and accountability
Strong strategic, analytical, and problem‑solving capabilities, with the ability to synthesize complex information into clear, executive‑ready recommendations
Exceptional communication and influencing skills, with experience engaging, advising, and constructively challenging senior leaders
Demonstrated ability to lead through ambiguity, manage multiple priorities, and deliver results in a fast‑paced, global environment

scope.surface: Global distribution strategy and operations for State Street Investment Management's Client Coverage Group (CCG), spanning Americas, EMEA, and APAC
scope.business_model: B2B asset management / institutional investor distribution
primary_metrics_inferred: OKRs and KPIs tracking progress against strategic objectives
cross_functional_partners: Head of CCG Strategy and Operations
core_responsibilities: Partner with CCG senior leadership to define, execute, and optimize global distribution strategy
Own and drive execution of complex, global distribution initiatives
Establish and manage key performance indicators (including OKRs)
Partner with regional leads on annual and ongoing business planning and commercial strategy development
Drive business process optimization and cross-business initiatives
Act as a recognized enterprise voice of CCG's distribution strategy

green_flags: Clear articulation of the role's scope and cross-functional partners
High-visibility role reporting to Head(s) of CCG and Head of CCG Strategy and Operations
Global remit across Americas, EMEA, and APAC adds breadth
red_flags: Wide compensation band ($115K-$205K) creates ambiguity about where a candidate would land
Role appears to be onsite in Boston based on job URL; no explicit remote or hybrid language in JD body
10+ years hard requirement with no stated flexibility or equivalent experience path

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from VP title in role name, 10+ years requirement, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false from 'senior individual contributor or player/coach' language; no direct people management stated)
location.type (inferred as onsite from city in URL and absence of any remote/hybrid language in JD body)

scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not fully enumerated in JD)

ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as onsite, hybrid, or remote in the JD body; inferred as onsite from Boston city in URL. Actual flexibility unclear.

The role title says VP but the JD does not clarify whether this is a people-manager VP or an individual-contributor VP; language suggests IC/player-coach but not definitive.
